plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-515 RP17.P01.M02.S09-RP17.P01.M04.S06 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-515 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP17.P01 implementation slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (the twelve remaining rows), the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first six rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H17), Claude review packet (C17), and CP00-516 handoff, without opening AI governance, model policy, retrieval scope, or citation runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: Independent read-only review of CP00-515 (RP17 AI Governance, P01 implementation slice; 40 units; Risk B; descriptor-only). The pack-level posture is correctly NON-production: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor", status="implemented_pending_review"; the "ai_governance_core_p01_implementation_slice_descriptor_verified" flag is a descriptor-verification flag, not a runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so no production_ready gate is weakened. Coverage is fully supported: 40 units (M02=12, M03=22, M04=6), deliverable distribution implementation:23/ui:5/fixture:2/test:6/hermes_evidence:2/claude_review:2, range RP17.P01.M02.S09-RP17.P01.M04.S06, matching the baseline, closeout-pack-plan.json (pack at line 407), and the program contract. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are pervasively attested in service.js/validators.js, manifest implementation_summary, and command-evidence (no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false); the source is pure frozen-object descriptor generation and the test only reads files. Hermes patterns are evidence/descriptor-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Claude remains read-only and is not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false; human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim=true), with the CP178+ hardening forbidden-evidence list embedded and the auth preflight passing with valid JSON (1434 bytes, exit 0). Source<->contract<->plan are internally consistent: the RP17 validator deep-equals the contract's descriptor/attestation projections and historical_pack_bindings tail is CP00-515. Required post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expected-absent at this pre-review stage. One P3 cosmetic finding: the CP515 descriptor result object carries RP16-style runtime-dispatch labels rather than RP17 AI-governance labels; it is non-blocking because every flag is false and the semantically-correct AI-governance no-dispatch attestation is present and enforced in the nested no_write_attestation. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Verdict PASS_WITH_FINDINGS; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: CP515 descriptor result uses RP16-style runtime-dispatch labels instead of RP17 AI-governance labels
findings.details: freezeCp515Result (service.js lines ~195-198) tags the descriptor result with dispatches_governance_runtime/dispatches_dlp_runtime/dispatches_retention_runtime/dispatches_legal_hold_runtime. Those are data-governance/DLP/retention/legal-hold (RP16-style) runtime names, not RP17 AI Governance scope (model policy, retrieval scope, audit, citation). The CP514 counterpart freezeCp514Result (lines 27-37) correctly uses dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/dispatches_model_policy_runtime/dispatches_citation_runtime/dispatches_retrieval_scope_runtime. Because the RP17 validator deep-equals the contract against this result, the same mislabeled fields are mirrored into contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json p01_implementation_slice_descriptor (lines 3012-3015). Impact is cosmetic/descriptor-hygiene only: every flag is false (safe direction), and the semantically-correct AI-governance no-dispatch attestation (dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/model_policy/citation/retrieval_scope = false) IS present in the nested no_write_attestation and enforced by the contract, manifest implementation_summary, and model.test.js. The no-write/no-runtime boundary is intact; the labels do not misrepresent or weaken any gate.
findings.recommendation: Rename the four top-level dispatch flags in freezeCp515Result to dispatches_ai_governance_runtime/dispatches_model_policy_runtime/dispatches_retrieval_scope_runtime/dispatches_citation_runtime to match freezeCp514Result and the RP17 program scope, then regenerate the contract projection so the validator deep-equal stays green. Optionally add an assertion that result-level dispatch labels match the program scope to prevent future template copy-paste drift. Non-blocking; suitable for a follow-up pack.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-515 ai governance core p01 implementation slice descriptor pack
